title: I'm 28M, never did intraday in my life, confused about how to use AI for trades, Please share your experiences
author: Crazy-Pizza8119
selftext:
Hi Guys, I'm 28M, never did intraday in my life but people in my social circle do it without learning a bit but by using AI and they make hell lot of profits.  
  
I have the idea, AI can speed up research, summarize news, and surface signals and but at the end of the day, the decision and risk management are still on me.

So I’m trying to understand how people are *actually* using it and where do you NOT trust AI ever at any cost?

Please share your experiences...

comments.author: epicskyes
comments.body:
I don’t trust ai to do anything but help me come up with ideas. Best thing is to use ai for strategy research. I used finance gpt 5.4 to build rule based strategies based on hard math.  40 core strategies with immutable hard math, indicator sets, built strict core schemas like this added tuning knobs for parameter variations and above that a rule based regime filter so only certain strategies can trade based on market conditions. 
  flow_volume:
    adl_ema_10: 'EMA_10(adl)_t'
    adl_high_20_prev: 'rolling_max(adl_(t-1), window=20)'
    adl_low_20_prev: 'rolling_min(adl_(t-1), window=20)'
    buy_pressure_10: 'up_vol_sum_10 / max(down_vol_sum_10, epsilon)'
    close_location_value: '(C_t - L_t) / max(H_t - L_t, epsilon)'
    clv: '((C_t - L_t) - (H_t - C_t)) / max(H_t - L_t, epsilon)'
    down_vol_sum_10: 'rolling_sum(V_t * 1{C_t < C_(t-1)}, 10)'
    pvt: 'cumulative_sum(V_t * (C_t / C_(t-1) - 1))'
    pvt_cross_down: 'pvt_ema_5_t < pvt_ema_20_t and pvt_ema_5_(t-1) >= pvt_ema_20_(t-1)'
    pvt_cross_up: 'pvt_ema_5_t > pvt_ema_20_t and pvt_ema_5_(t-1) <= pvt_ema_20_(t-1)'
    pvt_ema_20: 'EMA_20(pvt)_t'
    pvt_ema_5: 'EMA_5(pvt)_t'
    rvol_20: 'V_t / max(rolling_mean(V, 20), epsilon)'
    sell_pressure_10: 'down_vol_sum_10 / max(up_vol_sum_10, epsilon)'
    up_vol_sum_10: 'rolling_sum(V_t * 1{C_t > C_(t-1)}, 10)'
    volume_ratio_1_20: 'same as rvol_20'

replies.author: epicskyes
replies.body: No that’s the strategy math it uses on  built features to derive entry and exit signals

replies.author: epicskyes
replies.body: This is the sim I’m building this isn’t implemented yet. I’ve spent maybe 200 hours building the data qc/cleaning/features/normalization(news/divedends/earnings masking) runtime parameter/immutable strategy math/indicator alignment/logging/validation tests for every step/gui metrics localhost browser. I’m probably going to actually be able to run sims this week

comments.author: Jabba_au
comments.body:
Hey mate here is mine.


After months of work my AI trading bot is in the green

Now averging a modest $40 per day from $800 investment

I'm a finance broker by trade, and I got tired of manually watching charts. So I built an automated system using AI (OpenClaw + Python) that:

• Scans every USDT pair on Binance with >$5M volume

• Uses 4H klines with RSI(14), 20-period breakout, and volume spike confirmation

• Enters with market orders, 40% position sizing, max 2 concurrent

• Exits with layered take-profits: 20% at +30% (stop to breakeven), 20% at +50% (20% trail), 20% at +100% (10% trail)

• Kill switch at 50% drawdown, daily loss limit 20%

• Adaptive learning: adjusts entry thresholds every 10 trades based on win rate

The interesting part is the adaptive learning if win rate drops below 35%, it tightens entry filters. Above 60%, it loosens them. Simple feedback loop but it keeps the bot aligned with market conditions.

I wrote up the full strategy, code, and deployment process: myclawtrade.com

Happy to answer questions about the approach.

comments.author: No-Consequence-1779
comments.body:
You will need to do more reading on ai implementations and the problem set they are supposed to solve. And how it was already solved before ai. LLMs specifically.  

Then what are you going to do? Learn Python or another language and start writing stuff? It can get time consuming and involved. Likely with no or little improvement over your existing system.

comments.author: Ok-Programmer-3029
comments.body: I will not use ai for trades let’s link together im actually looking for a trading buddy

comments.body: Yo con mi chatgpt sacando 2% en cada trade sin complicarme la vida 😌

comments.author: jabberw0ckee
comments.body:
I built a service that alerts on high performing stocks. Universe changes every two weeks. 3 channels. 82% - 89% win rate.

Each alert also includes Analyst Ratings. Price Targets, News, Support and Resistance Levels, Chart Formations, and an AI Trading Advisor. 

You can paper trade the alerts in the free account. Starts with $10k. Double your money and get 30 days of Pro free. Double again and you get 30 days of Premium free. Save the free days until you’re ready to trade with $$.

comments.author: Helpful-Cut-4903
comments.body:
this is the right question to ask. AI is useful for research and narrowing things down. not for blindly copying trades now.

i’ve been using finnext: ai stock forecasts more like a filter for setups and context. then deciding myself from there

comments.author: Gelvandorf
comments.body: AI can help you come up with strategies and code in Python to backtest them.  It isn't just going to do everything for you, not without a lot of upfront work on your end.  You are still going to have to direct it and come up with ideas to test or choose combinations of strategies it suggests to test.  But it absolutely can help you create custom algorithms to program a bit with.  It's just very unlikely going to be able to do anything meaningful on its own.

comments.author: AdditionalClimate952
comments.body: Starting out is definitely a lot less stressful when you treat AI like a research assistant rather than a "get rich quick" button. What part of using AI for your trades feels the most confusing or risky to you right now?

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: Your intuition is 100% correct. the decision and risk management should never be fully handed over to an AI. Most people who claim to be making 'hell lot of profits' without learning anything are usually just riding a lucky streak in a bull market, and they’re one flash-crash away from losing it all. I use AI purely as a research assistant to cut down the hours of manual chart-watching. I’ve been using signalwhisper  lately because it doesn't trade for me; it just scans the macro sentiment and technicals to give me a high-probability 'whisper' of where the move is going. I still have to decide the entry and set my own stop losses. Don't trust any AI that asks for your exchange API keys or promises guaranteed returns. Use it for the data, but stay in the driver's seat.
